summary: Fast iteration already has useful narrow gates, but the common latency pressure is still cold native backend work and duplicated check/run verifier work. The best next implementation slice should keep full verification unchanged and make one narrow fast path cheaper or more selectable.

notes: Runs two parallel setup checks, then a long sequential chain that includes selfhost, source-run cache, native/runtime/swarm/monitoring scenarios, verify harness tests, example verifiers, and benchmark tests.
Supports CLASP_VERIFY_REPORT_JSON timing reports, CLASP_VERIFY_PARALLEL_COMMANDS, CLASP_VERIFY_SEQUENTIAL_COMMANDS, CLASP_VERIFY_FALLBACK_COMMANDS, and nested fallback behavior.
Wraps verify-all with a shorter command set but still includes selfhost, source-run cache, native claspc/runtime, compiler slices, runtime slices, and verifier harness regressions.
Good first worker gate because it selects existing targeted scripts from changed files and avoids the full verify-all surface for checkpoint/report and many example/runtime changes.
Each selected slice performs both claspc --json check and claspc run for the same fixture, then asserts output shape.
Live mode records source-run cold/warm, compiler-slice-checker, and native-incremental-body-change with explicit timeout wrappers. Fixture mode is the cheap regression path.
Fast mode now checks a small fixture through the promoted compiler image; full mode remains the expensive promoted-vs-rebuilt comparison path.

evidence: The cold trace shows bundle miss, run-binary build, native-image miss, build-plan miss, decl-module misses, and decl-module summary-plan work.
runtime/claspc.rs:7218 starts a run-binary fast path before bundle building; runtime/claspc.rs:7378 builds the cached backend binary when that path misses.
observed_bottlenecks.why_it_matters: Ordinary Clasp agent-loop verifier paths rely on claspc run. Cold cache behavior dominates first-run manager iteration while warm cache behavior is already cheap.

evidence: scripts/verify-compiler-slice.sh:141 runs claspc --json check and scripts/verify-compiler-slice.sh:147 then runs claspc run for the same fixture.
verify-fast includes bash scripts/verify-compiler-slice.sh all, so this duplicated work is in the common fast verifier path.
observed_bottlenecks.why_it_matters: The compiler slice verifier is valuable, but check-plus-run for every slice is costly when a worker only needs syntax/checker feedback or changed one compiler area.

evidence: benchmarks/checkpoints/2026-05-20-baseline-bottleneck.json recorded nativeImageCold=28.79s and checkCold=15.01s inside native-incremental-body-change.
runtime/claspc.rs:2409 load_cached_or_execute_native_image_build_plan and runtime/claspc.rs:2696 execute_parallel_module_decl_section_export are the key build-plan and decl-module cache paths.
runtime/claspc.rs:6373 module summary caching is already strong for warm and promoted-cache cases; edited large modules still fall back to semantic work.
observed_bottlenecks.why_it_matters: This is a real bottleneck, but it is larger and riskier than the first two targets because it touches cache correctness across native image and checker summaries.

bounded_implementation_targets.target: Add a cheaper compiler-slice mode and route only changed compiler areas to it.
bounded_implementation_targets.shape: Keep the existing default check-plus-run behavior. Add an explicit check-only or selected-slice fast mode for pre-full worker feedback, then have verify-affected select the smallest relevant slice for src/Compiler/* edits before verify-fast/full integration.
smallest_verification_commands: bash -n scripts/verify-compiler-slice.sh scripts/verify-fast.sh scripts/test-verify-compiler-slice.sh scripts/test-verify-affected.sh scripts/test-verify-all.sh

bounded_implementation_targets.target: Make the source-run cold/warm cache probe expose the next actionable miss instead of only pass/fail.
files_and_functions: runtime/claspc.rs:7218 run_main pre-bundle Run fast path
bounded_implementation_targets.shape: Do not change run semantics. Add a bounded cache trace assertion or checkpoint field that separates bundle miss, run-binary miss, native-image miss, build-plan miss, and decl-module miss for source-run-cold so the next cache optimization can be measured without rerunning verify-all.

commands_to_avoid_in_inner_loop: Unbounded claspc check or claspc run on src/Main.clasp
Live node scripts/benchmark-checkpoint.mjs with native incremental enabled unless the cache path itself changed
commands_run: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); for (const p of ['agents/feedback/wave1-baseline-fast-iteration-map.json','/home/akul_medexfinance_com/clasp/.clasp-loops/state/best-language-swarm-live-20260521041702/mailbox.json']) JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); console.log('json ok')"
residual_risks: This explorer slice intentionally did not implement the fast-verifier change.

Existing worktree changes from an earlier attempt remain present in scripts/benchmark-checkpoint.mjs, scripts/test-benchmark-checkpoint.sh, and benchmarks/checkpoints/2026-05-21-wave1-agent-readiness-probe.json; this report does not depend on reverting them.
The 2026-05-20 bottleneck checkpoint was captured in a sibling workspace, so exact timings should be treated as directional rather than as a fresh measurement for every host.
